primary_reason: The study describes the clinical features, genotypes, risk factors, and control experience of one mpox hospital cluster without identifying a reusable analytic or surveillance method.

The study combines a household mortality survey, p-scores, segmented Poisson regression, and algorithmic cause-of-death attribution to estimate pandemic excess mortality despite incomplete registration.
The study evaluates sequential rapid-test and agglutination testing to detect missed leptospirosis cases.

The cross-sectional survey describes physicians' knowledge, attitudes, and reported barriers concerning communicable-disease notification without developing or validating a reusable measurement approach.
The study cross-references patient-insurance records with national quality registers to expose incomplete capture of postoperative infections across reporting systems.
The review summarizes Chagas disease epidemiology, transmission routes, burden uncertainties, and control needs without substantively comparing or prescribing burden-estimation methods.
The study describes reported rape patterns and forensic findings, while its mention of sexually transmitted infections does not supply a concrete infectious-disease analytic transfer route.

The retrospective cohort estimates associations between COVID-19 exposure and autoimmune blistering diseases using standard survival analysis without a concrete infectious-disease methods contribution.
The mixed-methods evaluation triangulates household surveys, case verification, facility data, costs, interviews, and routine reports to assess community-based infectious-disease surveillance.
The three-case series illustrates clinical misdiagnosis of ocular sporotrichosis but does not evaluate a diagnostic rule or reusable ascertainment method.

The paper develops an iterative least-squares procedure for fitting discrete-time SIR models and diagnoses weak identification of transmission and persistence parameters using observed and simulated incidence.
The cluster-randomized survey integrates human serology, mosquito-pool PCR, spatial coverage, and longitudinal vector surveillance to characterize dengue transmission during pandemic disruption.
The analysis uses a long-running multisource regional system to measure trends in invasive pneumococcal disease, vaccine-serotype replacement, missing serotyping, and pandemic underreporting.
The porcine comparison demonstrates that a regulatory surface-sampling standard substantially underestimates dermal bacterial burden relative to full-thickness sampling and microscopy.
The chart review reports hepatitis B vaccination records and sparse antibody testing among athletes without developing a transferable vaccination-measurement method.
The pilot combines community alerts, field diagnostics, and viral sequencing to improve ascertainment and geographic interpretation of rabies circulation in underserved communities.
The systematic review uses proportional meta-analysis with generalized linear mixed models and inclusion rules for zero-event studies to estimate hepatitis B reactivation incidence.
The study compares a community survey with official dashboard data to assess COVID-19 underascertainment, vaccination access, and registration barriers in an Indigenous population.
The study adjusts population surveillance mortality using moving underreporting rates, demographic weighting, geospatial strata, and an optimal-survival benchmark.
The review concerns conservative treatment of a noninfectious musculoskeletal compartment syndrome and provides no concrete transfer route to infectious-disease epidemiology.
The study develops genetic frequency metrics and latent immunity models for epidemic-growth forecasting.
The review diagnoses tick-borne encephalitis surveillance failures and prescribes standardized clinical practice and wider laboratory access to reduce regional underreporting.
The pictorial essay provides imaging-based diagnostic guidance for recognizing dengue complications and differential diagnoses in emergency and critical-care settings.
The human seroprevalence component applies age-stratified antibody measurement to infer pertussis circulation.
The paper develops and validates Bayesian symbolic regression for learning parsimonious real-time incidence corrections from reported cases and testing rates across countries.
The study combines a nationally representative serosurvey with Bayesian geostatistical modeling to estimate and map hepatitis E infection beyond routine surveillance.
The outbreak investigation combines exposure evidence with isolate characterization and international genomic comparison to identify a foodborne EIEC source and transmission context.
The multinational study uses age- and cause-specific demographic decomposition to separate direct COVID-19 mortality from indirect pandemic changes in life expectancy.
The narrative review summarizes social barriers to cancer referrals among people with HIV but does not develop or compare a transferable infectious-disease method.
The review broadly summarizes salmonellosis epidemiology, diagnosis, resistance, and control recommendations without substantive methodological comparison or development.
The systematic review synthesizes incidence and diagnostic evidence and develops clinical algorithms.

The scoping review systematically maps pandemic effects on malaria programs into surveillance, case-management, vector-control, prevention, and program-management domains.
The study performs routine pharmacovigilance for psoriasis therapies without an infectious-disease methodological contribution.

The study integrates serological testing, viral sequencing, phylogenetic analysis, and cross-border comparisons to reconstruct measles importation and contact transmission.
This clinician-oriented vaccination review does not contribute a transferable epidemiological method.

The study compares multiple machine-learning models and uses discrepancies between predicted and reported COVID-19 incidence to flag probable geographic underreporting.
The review and expert round table evaluate respiratory diagnostic gaps and prescribe target populations and implementation roles for rapid molecular point-of-care testing.
